title: Metaplex createV1 called with isMutable: false to seal token metadata at mint

params.passDetail: createV1 passes isMutable: false — token metadata is permanently sealed at mint time.
params.failAbsentDetail: createV1 omits isMutable (defaults to true). The update authority retains the ability to change the token name, symbol, and URI after mint — permanently. Seal the metadata by passing isMutable: false.
params.failWrongDetail: createV1 passes isMutable: true explicitly. Token metadata will remain mutable after mint — the update authority key becomes a permanent attack surface. Pass isMutable: false to seal it.
params.failArgDetail: createV1's options argument is not an inline object literal; cannot statically verify isMutable. Inline the options object so static analysis can confirm isMutable: false.
params.failDynamicDetail: isMutable is set via a variable or expression; cannot confirm it resolves to false statically. Use the literal false.
params.absentCallDetail: Handler is in Metaplex scope but does not call createV1; the metadata-immutability rule does not apply here.
params.scopeNotMetDetail: File does not import from @metaplex-foundation/mpl-token-metadata; rule does not apply.

provenance.note: isMutable defaults to true in the Metaplex Umi SDK. Setting it false is a one-shot, irrevocable decision — there is no on-chain migration path once metadata is minted as mutable.
